docket_entries.raw_text: CLERK'S NOTICE SETTING CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE RE DOCKET NO. 14 .Notice is hereby given that a Case Management Conference has been set via zoom for June 23, 2026, before Judge Haywood S. Gilliam, Jr., at 2:00 p.m. All attorneys and pro se litigants appearing for the case management conference are required to join at least 15 minutes before the hearing to check-in with the CRD. Webinar Access: All counsel, members of the public, and media may access the webinar information at https://www.cand.uscourts.gov/hsg Civ LR 77-3(d). Persons granted access to court proceedings held by telephone or videoconference are reminded that photographing, recording, and rebroadcasting of court proceedings, including screenshots or other visual copying of a hearing, is absolutely prohibited.Zoom Guidance and Setup: https://cand.uscourts.gov/zoom. (This is a text-only entry generated by the court. There is no document associated with this entry.)(ndr, COURT STAFF) (Filed on 6/4/2026) (Entered: 06/04/2026)
docket_entries.summary: The court scheduled a case management conference for June 23, 2026, at 2:00 p. m. before Judge Haywood S. Gilliam, Jr. , to be held via Zoom. The notice requires attorneys to join 15 minutes early for check-in and prohibits recording or photographing the proceedings.

Kevin Castel on 5/26/2026) (vfr) (Entered: 05/26/2026)

name: Kogon v. Google, LLC

key_rulings.description: The court exercised its discretion to stay discovery until resolution of the defendant's anticipated motion to dismiss.
key_rulings.summary: The court struck a previously scheduled status hearing after reviewing a joint status report, noting that the defendant intends to file a motion to dismiss. The court granted the defendant's request for additional pages (up to 30 pages) for the motion and stayed all discovery until the motion to dismiss is resolved.

docket_entries.raw_text: STATUS Report Joint Initial Status Report by Attack the Sound LLC, James Burjek, Stan Burjek, Berk Ergoz, Magnus Fiennes, Hamza Jilani, Sam Kogon, Michael Mell, Arjun Singh, Maatkara Wilson, David Woulard (Kimbarovsky, Ross) (Entered: 05/27/2026)
docket_entries.summary: Multiple parties filed a joint initial status report, which is typically used to inform the court about case management issues, discovery plans, and scheduling preferences early in litigation.

docket_entries.raw_text: MINUTE entry before the Honorable Sunil R. Harjani: Upon review of the joint initial status report 19, the status hearing previously set for 6/3/2026 is stricken. Defendant intends to file a motion to dismiss. At the parties' request, the Court will grant additional pages for the motion up to 30 pages only. The Court exercises its broad discretion to stay discovery in this case until resolution of the motion to dismiss and until all parties and this Court know which claims will go forward. Mailed notice (lxs, ) (Entered: 06/01/2026)
docket_entries.summary: The court struck a previously scheduled status hearing after reviewing a joint status report, noting that the defendant intends to file a motion to dismiss. The court granted the defendant's request for additional pages (up to 30 pages) for the motion and stayed all discovery until the motion to dismiss is resolved.
